spec_match.rationale: The lab now uses explicit anchor-name identifiers and current position-try-order fallback ordering.

interactive.rationale: Users can switch CSS and JS modes, scroll the stage, focus or hover terms, and inspect layout-read counts.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The tooltip lab now demonstrates the remembered-scroll-offset use case rather than anchor positioning alone.

edge_cases.rationale: The page covers explicit anchor identifiers, deep scroll positions, fallback ordering, and JS fallback costs.

references.rationale: The missing see-also block now links to ChromeStatus, the spec, and sibling concepts.

design_system.rationale: The page uses the shared design variables and no raw color literals.

summary: The tooltip lab now validates explicit anchor names, current fallback ordering, and the CSS-vs-JS comparison promised by the page.
